old_claim.note: Advanced Materials Science provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency remains because deep drilling and directional drilling draw from petroleum drilling practice, electronics, survey tools, mud motors, and drill-string mechanics rather than the postwar materials-science discipline itself.

source_shape.source_locator: Directional Drilling of Boreholes section describing petroleum directional-drilling roots, 1950s drainhole work, borehole assemblies, surveying, buckling, and drill-string mechanics.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The National Academies review treats directional and deep drilling as petroleum-derived drilling, survey, steering, and mechanical systems rather than as an application that requires a materials-science discipline node.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: That source explains the relevant technology lineage without invoking postwar materials science as a prerequisite; keeping the edge would also force a later 1950 field label into a 1945-era drilling node.

ontology_before: Deep drilling carried a broad advanced-materials-science prerequisite, creating chronology debt after the materials-science node was scoped to postwar MSE.
ontology_after: Deep drilling keeps direct dependencies on oil drilling and electronics while materials improvements remain unmodeled contextual improvements unless a narrower drill-materials node is added later.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Graph invariant 2026-06-09-transport-drilling-materials-chronology.json keeps this direct edge absent.
would_reject_if: The deep drilling node were rescoped to a later high-temperature drill-materials platform after 1950.
A drilling-history source showed postwar materials science was a necessary direct dependency rather than a contextual improvement.
The graph added a narrower drill-string alloys or high-pressure downhole-materials node and retargeted this dependency there.
short_reason: Postwar materials science is too broad and too late to be a direct dependency for this drilling node.
